headline: Anthropic reversed a policy that would have silently degraded Claude for frontier AI development, drawing public criticism even as pricing pressure with OpenAI intensified.
body: Anthropic walked back a policy that could have secretly limited Claude on frontier LLM tasks without notifying users, conceding it made the wrong transparency tradeoff, while Redis creator Antirez and others publicly condemned the company's approach. Pricing strategy moved in parallel: OpenAI is weighing price cuts to compete with Anthropic for users, and one user flagged the Fable model burning 45 percent of a weekly quota in a single day. On the technique side RLHF leads the rotation and also breaks out, alongside speculative decoding, attention mechanism, multi-turn reasoning, rejection sampling, and human control. Agent reliability surfaced as a thread, with one AI agent reportedly running amok in Fedora and datasette-agent adding mandatory human approval for stored queries.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
Antirez on X: I believe what Anthropic is doing is *deeply* wrong
OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ - CNBC
Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
AI agent runs amok in Fedora and elsewhere
